risk_summary: Security concerns are contributing to delays in contract implementation and construction activities across project sites.
evidence_quote: Security issue, rainy season, delay in effecting compensation and delay in site handover to the contractors are also contributed for the delay.

risk_title: Weak Contract Administration and Monitoring Due to Governance Gaps
risk_summary: Poor coordination between regional and central authorities (MoWE) in contract administration has created governance gaps that undermine project oversight and contractor accountability.
evidence_quote: The contract administration arrangement between the regions and MoWE created a gap in properly supervising the Works contracts. Generally, the mission identified that weak contract monitoring and close follow up by all parties involved is a critical issue that requires immediate action and improve the contract administration system of the project.

risk_title: Staffing Vacancies at Regional and Local Levels Affecting Implementation
risk_summary: Unfilled staff positions at town and regional levels are hampering project implementation capacity, particularly in environmental and social safeguards, sanitation engineering, and monitoring roles.
evidence_quote: However, the staffing vacancies at the town and regional levels have not been addressed. It is recommended that MoWE, in consultation with the regions, urgently fills the required staff positions.

risk_title: Delayed Resettlement and Land Acquisition Processes
risk_summary: Involuntary resettlement processes are significantly delayed across multiple project sites, with RAP/ARAP preparation incomplete for numerous towns, creating risks of project delays and community tensions.
evidence_quote: MoWE must complete the preparation of RAP for the Arba Minch FSTP addressing WB's team comments, initiate or complete RAP/ARAP preparation for the construction of FSTPs in Adigrat, Assela, Bishoftu, Dessie, Gondar, Jigjiga, Jimma, Shashemene and the five towns (Hawassa, Bahir Dar, Adama, Dire Dawa & Mekele), and expedite the implementation of ARAPs for the Harar, Assosa, Nekemte and Debrebirhan towns.

risk_title: Governance Fragility in Utility Management and Coordination
risk_summary: Lack of clear role delineation between municipalities and water and sanitation service enterprises (WSSEs) is undermining effective project governance and service delivery coordination.
evidence_quote: There is no clear role delineation between municipality and WSSE

risk_summary: While GRMs have been established, their functionality remains weak across implementing entities, limiting the ability to effectively address community grievances and potential conflicts.
evidence_quote: Strengthen the functionality of the local level GRMs to effectively record, resolve, monitor and regularly report any grievances related to resettlement and any other project activities.
